reasoning: In Mathlib, `AddSubgroup.index` is defined as `Nat.card (G ⧸ H)`, which returns `0` when the quotient is infinite (confirmed by the lemma `index_eq_zero_iff_infinite : H.index = 0 ↔ Infinite (G ⧸ H)`). Thus `H.index = 0` precisely encodes "H has infinite index". The Lean statement asserts that every additive subgroup H ≠ ⊤ of ℚ satisfies H.index = 0, i.e., every proper subgroup has infinite index. This is logically equivalent to the informal claim "ℚ has no proper subgroups of finite index" (contrapositive: a subgroup of finite index must equal ⊤). The hypothesis `H ≠ ⊤` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "proper subgroup" and correctly excludes only the case H = ℚ where index would be 1.
